output.final_deliverable:
Start at Wikipedia Main Page: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page

Chain of Wikipedia URLs (in order):
1) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page
2) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Featured_content
3) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Featured_article
4) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elizabeth_II
5) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Monarchy_of_the_United_Kingdom

Verified adjacency (evidence that URL[4] links to URL[5]):
- On the page for Elizabeth II (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elizabeth_II), the article contains a link to the Monarchy of the United Kingdom (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Monarchy_of_the_United_Kingdom), confirming the adjacency. See: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elizabeth_II -> https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Monarchy_of_the_United_Kingdom

Summary:
I started from the Main Page and followed links from Featured content -> Featured article -> Elizabeth II -> Monarchy of the United Kingdom. The adjacency between Elizabeth II and Monarchy of the United Kingdom is verified by the link present on the Elizabeth II article.
output.action_summary: Chain of 5 Wikipedia pages starting from Main Page, with one verified adjacency: Elizabeth II links to Monarchy of the United Kingdom.

b97f2a9b-9abe-47c3-a9aa-c30f28f5dab3.title:
Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).

Benchmark rule: when external evidence is needed, run search then visit the best URLs. Visit pages to extract concrete details and cite visited URLs in the final answer.

details.mandate:
Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).

Benchmark rule: when external evidence is needed, run search then visit the best URLs. Visit pages to extract concrete details and cite visited URLs in the final answer.

ae66c11f-311d-4893-97d3-0a63c131476c.title: Visit Wikipedia Main Page and build a 4+ URL chain by following in-page links (sequential plan)

details.intent: Start at the Wikipedia Main Page, extract internal (en.wikipedia.org) links from the page, choose a next page from those links, then visit that page and repeat until a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs is built. Verify one adjacency pair near the end by proving the earlier page contains a link to the later page (show the linking anchor / URL).
measurable_output_fields: chain_urls (ordered list of >=4 Wikipedia URLs starting at Main Page)
link_text_or_anchor_snippet_for_verified_pair (HTML or anchor text showing the link exists)
verified_pair_index (index i where chain_urls[i] links to chain_urls[i+1])
visit_snippets (short excerpt from each visited page showing the next URL or context used to choose next link)
target_facts: Each subsequent URL must be an en.wikipedia.org/wiki/... page discovered as an internal link on the previous visited page
At least 4 total URLs (so Main Page + at least 3 subsequent pages)
At least one adjacency pair (URL[i] -> URL[i+1]) must be verified by extracting a link element or text from the earlier page that points to the later page
details.extraction_plan: 1) Fetch the Main Page HTML/text. 2) Extract internal article links (hrefs that start with '/wiki/' and are not in the 'Special:', 'Talk:', 'Help:', 'File:' namespaces). 3) Select a next page candidate from that list using a simple deterministic rule (prefer first content link in the lead section that points to an article page). 4) Visit that next page URL (converted to full https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/...). 5) Repeat extraction to collect the next link. 6) Continue until chain length >= 4. 7) For one adjacency near the end (prefer the penultimate adjacency, e.g., chain[-2] -> chain[-1]), explicitly verify by locating the exact link element on the earlier page that references the later page and capture an excerpt: the anchor href and surrounding text or an HTML fragment showing the anchor. 8) Save visit snippets (short sentences or paragraphs) that justify why chosen link was selected and to document the context. 9) If at any point a chosen page has no eligible links in the content area (very unlikely), fall back to selecting the first eligible link from page 'Contents' or another content section; if still none, select a different earlier link from the parent page's candidate list.
verification_steps: After visiting each page, enumerate internal links and confirm the chosen next URL is present in the enumerated list.

For the verified adjacency pair, extract the exact anchor element (href value) and capture the line or small HTML snippet where it appears on the earlier page to prove the earlier page links to the next page.
Confirm all URLs are full https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/... links and resolve any relative hrefs accordingly.
Record a short excerpt around each found link to provide context and permit human verification.
details.why_this_reduces_uncertainty: Directly visiting pages and extracting in-page link hrefs removes ambiguity from search snippets. Searching only returns summaries; visiting provides the actual link elements needed to prove adjacency. Verifying adjacency by extracting the anchor element from the earlier page provides concrete evidence that the earlier page links to the later one.
details.selection_criteria_for_next_link: Prefer first internal link appearing in the lead/first paragraph that points to a main-namespace article (href starts with '/wiki/' and excludes namespaces like 'File:', 'Special:', 'Help:', 'Talk:'). This deterministic rule avoids arbitrary choices and simplifies reproducibility. If the lead has no eligible links (rare), use the first eligible link in the body content.
details.fallbacks_and_edge_cases: If the Main Page presents many navigation elements and the lead selection yields an administrative link, skip until an eligible article link is found. If any visited page is a redirect, resolve the redirect by visiting the resolved page and treat that resolved URL as the page in the chain.
details.planned_children_actions_summary: This node will: (A) visit Main Page and extract candidate links, (B) pick next page and visit it, (C) repeat until chain >=4, (D) for a chosen adjacency near the end extract anchor evidence that earlier page links to later page, (E) produce final outputs: chain (ordered list), verification evidence (anchor snippet), and short visit snippets for each step.

why_this_node_is_needed_and_how_it_contr.justification: The task requires visiting the starting URL and following on-page links to build a chain and provide one verified adjacency. A single, sequential visit node beginning at the provided Main Page URL is the correct starting point because the benchmark requires visiting pages (not just searching) to extract concrete link elements. This node defines the full, stepwise extraction/verification plan so child visits (executed sequentially by the orchestrator) will have clear selection rules, extraction targets, verification steps, measurable outputs, and fallback strategies. It thus converts the user's mandate into an executable sequence that yields the required chain and evidence.

details.constraints_and_assumptions: Assumes en.wikipedia.org is reachable and pages contain standard wiki internal links. Assumes redirects will be followed and resolved. Assumes the executor will run sequential visits and extract link hrefs from the HTML/text returned.
details.how_results_will_be_presented: Final output will be an ordered list of the chain URLs (starting with Main Page) and, near the end, a small extracted evidence block showing the anchor (href or small HTML snippet) from the earlier page that links to the later page, plus short visit snippets describing context for each step.
details.why_this_node: This single visit node encapsulates the entire sequential workflow starting from the provided URL, satisfying the benchmark's requirement to visit pages to extract concrete link evidence and produce a verifiable chain of Wikipedia URLs.
details.parent_goal:
Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).

Benchmark rule: when external evidence is needed, run search then visit the best URLs. Visit pages to extract concrete details and cite visited URLs in the final answer.
